critique.summary: OpenTTD is no longer available as a standalone game on Steam and can now only be obtained as part of a bundle with Transport Tycoon Deluxe. The game remains free, but new players must purchase the bundle to access it on Steam.
mainPoints: OpenTTD has transitioned from a standalone game on Steam to a bundled offering with Transport Tycoon Deluxe.
Existing players will not be affected and can still access updates and re-download the game.
critique.truthfulness: The article accurately describes the changes to OpenTTD's availability on Steam and clarifies that the game remains free, albeit requiring a purchase of the bundle for new players. The information aligns with the current distribution practices on Steam and GOG.
considerations: The article does not mention the potential impact on the player base due to the new purchasing requirement.
It does not provide insights into player reactions or feedback regarding the change.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the implications of OpenTTD's availability on Steam and Atari's involvement in the distribution. Users express mixed feelings about Atari's role and the quality of OpenTTD compared to the original Transport Tycoon Deluxe.

comments.author: ndiddy
comments.text: Obviously having OpenTTD available for free on Steam would jeopardize Atari's paid rerelease of Transport Tycoon Deluxe...
comments.analysis: This comment provides a well-reasoned perspective on the potential business implications for Atari and acknowledges the contributions of the OpenTTD team.

comments.author: TuxMark5
comments.text: This doesn't feel right for me. OpenTTD is so much superior in every way compared to the original TTD...
comments.analysis: While the comment expresses strong feelings about the situation, it lacks a balanced view of Atari's actions and motivations.

comments.author: koolala
comments.text: Atari got a game I like called Awesomenauts and revived it from being shutdown F2P to $20...
comments.analysis: This comment shares a personal experience with Atari, providing context but not directly addressing the main topic of OpenTTD.

comments.author: xyzzy_plugh
comments.text: It would be nice if someone could provide some explanation as to why this situation is necessary...
comments.analysis: The comment seeks clarification on the situation, which is a constructive approach, but lacks depth in analysis.

comments.author: jjmarr
comments.text: I like Simutrans more because the cargo and passengers have destinations in mind...
comments.analysis: This comment diverges from the main discussion about OpenTTD and Atari, focusing instead on personal preferences in transport games.

comments.author: nout
comments.text: What is the story with OpenGFX then? It sounds like OpenTTD is completely new codebase...
comments.analysis: The comment raises a relevant question about OpenGFX, but it does not contribute significantly to the overall discussion.

comments.author: xyzzy_plugh
comments.text: Weirdly the OpenTTD Steam page seems to be missing entirely at the moment...
comments.analysis: This comment points out an observation about the Steam page, but it lacks a deeper analysis of the implications.

comments.author: r2vcap
comments.text: Atari? I never expected to see that ancient name again. If I remember correctly, I've been playing OpenTTD for more than a decade...
comments.analysis: While the comment reflects on personal experience, it does not engage deeply with the core issues raised in the discussion.

comments.author: basilikum
comments.text: OpenTTD with its beyond clunky UI has a special spot in my heart...
comments.analysis: This comment shares a personal sentiment but does not contribute to the discussion about Atari or the distribution changes.

comments.author: sarru_kin
comments.analysis: This comment is overly simplistic and does not engage with the complexities of the topic at hand.

comments.author: ocdtrekkie
comments.text: This seems quite positive to me: Clearly the rightsholders are not being total jerks since they're happy to allow an OpenTTD bundle...
comments.analysis: While the comment offers a positive perspective, it may overlook potential concerns regarding Atari's motivations.
